content.text:
Perfect. Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive inventory.

---

## COMPLETE INVENTORY: intell-lexxi-backend

### 1. API ROUTES & ENDPOINTS

**Router Files:**

#### `/app/routes/ai_router.py` (prefix: `/ai`)
Primary AI and genomic interpretation endpoints. Includes 12 POST routes:

1. **`POST /ai/ai-concerns-chat`** - Streaming SSE conversation with Concerns Extractor agent (UC1). Accepts multipart form with messages JSON and optional file uploads (PDF/DOCX/TXT). Streams delta text, extracted concerns, and errors.

2. **`POST /ai/genomic-interpretation`** - Rules Engine v5 for genomic interpretation. Processes patient genotypes and selected concerns, outputs SNP results with odds ratios and facts.

3. **`POST /ai/prevalence-view`** - Prevalence View pipeline (pv-v1). Lists ALL low-prevalence SNP results in single table ordered by genotype frequency, independent of clinical concerns.

4. **`POST /ai/clinical-discussion`** - Clinical Discussion Agent. Generates clinician-oriented discussion for patient SNP results per concern using GPT-4o with SNP obfuscation ({SNP-X} placeholders).

5. **`POST /ai/clinical-discussion-stream`** - Streaming version of Clinical Discussion Agent. Returns SSE stream of discussion text chunks.

6. **`POST /ai/clinical-chat-stream`** - Streaming Clinical Chat Agent. Conversational interface for clinicians to ask follow-up questions about genomic results with full context.

7. **`POST /ai/clinician-summary-plan`** - Generates opening cards of patient-facing action plan: one Overview card plus one card per concern. Single LLM call with structured output.

8. **`POST /ai/interventions`** - Interventions Rules Engine. Resolves tiered interventions from R&D Tool data based on clinician-selected SNPs per concern.

9. **`POST /ai/action-plan-commentary-data`** - Assembles payload for action plan commentary. Builds selected SNPs and interventions structures for commentary generation.

10. **`POST /ai/action-plan-commentary`** - Generates patient-facing commentary from selected SNPs and interventions using Action Plan Commentary agent (gpt-5.4).

11. **`POST /ai/refresh-cache`** - Manually trigger cache reload of all reference data (tags, SNPs, interventions, studies, interactions).

12. **`POST /ai/chat-stream`** - Generic chat streaming endpoint. Used by UC6 (Chat interface).

#### `/app/routes/data_router.py` (prefix: `/data`)
Patient and genomic data queries:

1. **`GET /data/patients`** - Paginated list of patients with optional search and sorting. Query params: `search` (optional), `page` (1-based), `per_page` (1-100, default 10), `sort_order` (asc|desc). Returns `PaginatedResponse[Patient]`.

2. **`GET /data/patient/{patient_id}`** - Detailed patient information for a specific patient. Returns `Patient` model.

3. **`GET /data/patient/{patient_id}/results`** - Genetic results for a specific patient. Returns `PatientResults` containing gene_results array.

4. **`GET /data/concerns`** - All available clinical concerns. Returns `ConcernsResponse` with concerns array.

#### `/app/routes/misc_router.py`
Utility and health routes:

1. **`GET /health`** - Health check endpoint. Verifies PostgreSQL and OpenAI service availability. Returns `HealthCheckResponse`.

2. **`GET /sentry-debug`** - (Non-production only) Debug endpoint that triggers deliberate ZeroDivisionError to verify Sentry integration.

---

### 2. MLflow INTEGRATION

**File:** `/app/ai/mlflow_tracker.py` (21.6 KB comprehensive module)

**Architecture:**
- **Two independent flags:** `MLFLOW_ENABLED` (master switch) and `MLFLOW_TRACING_ENABLED` (distributed tracing only)
- **Auto-tracing:** Uses `mlflow.openai.autolog()` to capture every OpenAI/Agents SDK call with zero per-call instrumentation
- **Async trace I/O:** Non-blocking background thread for trace flushing (env: `MLFLOW_ENABLE_ASYNC_TRACE_LOGGING=true`)
- **Session-based hierarchy:** Parent `session_run()` groups related `agent_run()` children

**Core Functions:**

1. **`configure()`** - One-time startup initialization. Reads settings:
   - `mlflow_enabled`, `mlflow_tracking_uri`, `mlflow_experiment_name`, `mlflow_tracing_enabled`
   - Optional basic auth: `mlflow_username`, `mlflow_password` (writes to env vars)
   - Sets experiment tags (environment metadata) and enables autolog

2. **`session_run()`** - Context manager for parent MLflow runs. Groups all agent calls from one patient session under single collapsible entry in UI

3. **`agent_run(run_name, params, tags=None)`** - Context manager for individual agent invocation. Logs params, auto-records `latency_ms` on exit. Nests under active session_run when open. Yields mutable metrics dict

4. **`trace_span(name, attributes=None, request_preview=None)`** - Manual distributed tracing parent span. Groups auto-logged child spans. Links to HTTP correlation ID via `mlflow.trace.session` metadata key

5. **`update_trace_preview(response_preview)`** - Sets response preview text on active trace (shown in MLflow UI)

6. **`log_text(text, filename)`** - Log plain-text artifact to active run

7. **`log_dict(data, filename)`** - Log JSON artifact to active run

8. **`log_token_usage(result)`** - Extract and log token counts from OpenAI Agents SDK result. Logs `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `total_tokens` as metrics

9. **`ensure_prompt(name, template, commit_message="", tags=None)`** - Register prompt in MLflow Prompt Registry. Compares against latest version; creates new version only if text changed. Returns version number or None on failure

**Configuration (from app/core/config.py):**
- `mlflow_enabled: bool = True` (default enabled)
- `mlflow_tracing_enabled: bool = True` (default enabled)
- `mlflow_tracking_uri: str = "https://api-dev.ixxdna.com/mlflow/"`
- `mlflow_experiment_name: str = "lexxi-ai-agents"`
- `mlflow_username: str = ""` (optional)
- `mlflow_password: str = ""` (optional)

**Initialization:** Called in `app/main.py` lifespan during app startup. Also registers 4 agent system prompts:
- `init_clinical_discussion_prompt()` 
- `init_clinical_chat_prompt()`
- `init_clinician_summary_plan_prompt()`
- `init_concerns_extractor_prompt()`

**Agent Usage:** Imported and used in:
- `/app/ai/agent_extract_concern.py` - Concerns extractor agent (uses agent_run, log_token_usage, trace_span)
- `/app/ai/agent_clinical_discussion.py` - Clinical discussion (session_run, agent_run, trace_span, log_text, log_token_usage)
- `/app/ai/agent_clinical_chat.py` - Clinical chat (agent_run, log_text, log_token_usage)
- `/app/ai/agent_clinician_summary_plan.py` - Summary plan (agent_run, log_text, log_token_usage, trace_span)

---

### 3. ALL AI AGENTS/PIPELINES

**Directory:** `/app/ai/` contains 13 core files

#### **Agents (OpenAI Agents SDK-based, conversational):**

1. **`agent_extract_concern.py` (14.6 KB)**
   - **Purpose:** UC1 (Topic/Concern Selection) — Multi-turn conversational concern extraction from clinician input
   - **Features:** File attachments (PDF/DOCX/TXT), streaming SSE, structured concern output with source tags
   - **Model:** gpt-4o, temperature 0.7, max 1000 tokens
   - **Key Functions:**
     - `run_concerns_stream(conversation, available_concerns)` - Async generator streaming concern extraction
     - `init_prompt()` - Registers prompt in MLflow Registry on startup
   - **Source Tags:** `patient_concerns`, `family_history`, `genomic_results`
   - **Integration:** Uses mlflow_tracker for tracing and prompt registry

2. **`agent_clinical_discussion.py` (21.9 KB)**
   - **Purpose:** Generates clinician-oriented discussion for patient SNP results per concern
   - **Features:** SNP obfuscation ({SNP-X} placeholders), concurrent concern processing, streaming support
   - **Model:** gpt-4o, temperature 0.5, max 1200 tokens
   - **Key Functions:**
     - `run_discussions_concurrent(concerns_data, snps_by_rsid, cache)` - Process multiple concerns concurrently
     - `run_discussions_stream(message, concerns_data, snps_by_rsid, cache)` - Streaming variant
     - `init_prompt()` - Registers prompt at startup
   - **Post-processing:** Normalizes {SNP-X} placeholders, strips leaked rs_ids
   - **Integration:** session_run + agent_run nesting for parent/child run hierarchy

3. **`agent_clinical_chat.py` (16.1 KB)**
   - **Purpose:** Streaming conversational assistant for clinicians to ask follow-up questions about genomic results
   - **Features:** Context-aware (receives prior genomic interpretation + discussion), SNP obfuscation, streaming
   - **Model:** gpt-4o, temperature 0.5, max 1000 tokens
   - **Key Functions:**
     - `run_chat_stream(message, context, snps_by_rsid, tag_by_name)` - Async generator for chat stream
     - `init_prompt()` - Registers prompt
   - **Integration:** Shares obfuscation scheme with clinical_discussion

4. **`agent_clinician_summary_plan.py` (13 KB)**
   - **Purpose:** Generates opening cards of patient-facing action plan (Overview + per-concern cards)
   - **Features:** Single LLM call (faster than concurrent), global SNP mapping, structured output_type
   - **Model:** gpt-4o, temperature 0.5, max 900 tokens
   - **Key Functions:**
     - `run(concerns_data, snps_by_rsid, tag_by_name)` - Single Runner.run() with output_type
     - `init_prompt()` - Registers prompt
   - **Output:** Pydantic model with overview_card + concern_cards

5. **`agent_action_plan_commentary.py` (3.4 KB)**
   - **Purpose:** Generates patient-facing commentary from clinician-selected SNPs and interventions
   - **Features:** Pure AI layer, receives all data via payload
   - **Model:** gpt-5.4, temperature 0.2, max 1600 tokens
   - **Key Functions:**
     - `generate_action_plan_commentary_from_payload(payload)` - Single Runner.run()
   - **Post-processing:** Removes leaked rsIDs

#### **Pipelines (Deterministic Rules Engines — pure logic, no I/O):**

6. **`lexxi_pipeline_genomic_interpretation.py` (111 KB)**
   - **Purpose:** Core deterministic Rules Engine v5 for genomic interpretation
   - **Architecture:** Pure-logic class, zero DB calls. Same input → same output always.
   - **Rules Loaded:**
     - `rules/disabled_snps.json` - SNPs to filter out
     - `rules/fundamentals.json` - Fundamental concept synonyms
     - `rules/critical_snps.json` - SNPs not to miss
   - **Key Features:**
     - Tag walking (topic → associated_tags, depth 1)
     - SNP matching (unique_tags + facts.tags)
     - Prevalence from MAF (Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium)
     - 4 prioritization groups
     - Variant merging (same gene, within 10% prevalence)
     - Key Point (AI) fact extraction
     - SNP interactions support
     - Wellness special case
   - **Key Class:** `LexxiPipelineGenomicInterpretation(snps, tags, tag_by_id, patient)`
   - **References:** Lexxi Rulebook §5-9

7. **`lexxi_pipeline_interventions.py` (20.9 KB)**
   - **Purpose:** Rules Engine for resolving tiered interventions from selected SNPs per concern
   - **Architecture:** Pure logic, zero I/O, deterministic
   - **Data Model:** Maps SNP.tier_information_by_tag[tag_id] → interventions with tier levels
   - **Key Class:** `LexxiPipelineInterventions(snps, interventions, ...)`
   - **Output:** `InterventionsResponse` with per-concern intervention tiers
   - **References:** Lexxi Rulebook §12 (Intervention Engine)

8. **`lexxi_pipeline_prevalence_view.py` (23.8 KB)**
   - **Purpose:** Prevalence View pipeline (pv-v1) — ALL low-prevalence SNPs in single table
   - **Architecture:** Pure logic, decoupled from genomic_interpretation
   - **Key Differences:** No concerns, no tag walking, no 4 selection rules, selection by prevalence ascending
   - **Features:** Matches highest-OR fact per SNP, filtered by disabled SNPs
   - **Key Class:** `LexxiPipelinePrevalenceView(snps, ...)`
   - **Output:** `PrevalenceViewResponse` with SNP results ordered by prevalence
   - **References:** Lexxi UX §5

#### **Data Services & Cache:**

9. **`reference_data_cache.py` (19.7 KB)**
   - **Purpose:** In-memory cache of all R&D Tool reference data (tags, SNPs, interventions, studies, interactions)
   - **Features:**
     - Lock-protected reload (in-flight requests serve previous snapshot while reload in progress)
     - Reconstructs nested document shapes from PostgreSQL normalized tables
     - Two-phase parallel loading (studies+tags first, then SNPs+interventions+interactions)
     - Memory metrics via Sentry: cache.snp_count, cache.tag_count, cache.study_count, cache.intervention_count, cache.snp_interaction_count
   - **Key Methods:**
     - `ensure_loaded()` - Pre-load all data (idempotent)
     - `reload()` - Full reload without serving partial state
   - **Loaded Entities:** Tags, SNPs, SnpDetails, SnpInteractions, Interventions, Studies
   - **Provides:** `all_snps`, `tag_by_name`, `tag_by_id`, `all_interventions`, `all_studies`, `snp_interactions`

10. **`ai_data_service.py` (2.8 KB)**
    - **Purpose:** Patient-specific data access layer (async queries for patient records, genotypes, age)
    - **Key Methods:**
      - `get_patient_by_id(patient_id)` - Fetch patient record with dob as datetime
      - `get_patient_genotypes(patient_id)` - Get gene_results as gene_id → {genotype, result_value, prevalence} dict
      - `get_patient_age(patient)` - Calculate age from date_of_birth
    - **DB Entity Mapping:** Patient, PatientResult via SQLAlchemy ORM

#### **Supporting:**

11. **`cache_refresh_task.py` (1.7 KB)**
    - **Purpose:** Periodic background task for reloading reference data cache
    - **Class:** `CacheRefreshTask` — asyncio-based periodic task manager
    - **Default Interval:** 300 seconds (5 minutes)
    - **Lifecycle:** `start()` schedules task, `stop()` cancels and waits cleanly
    - **Usage:** Created in app lifespan, runs during app lifetime

12. **`chat.py` (1.1 KB)**
    - **Purpose:** Generic chat functionality wrapper (minimal, likely legacy or utility)

13. **`ai/__init__.py` (79 bytes)**
    - **Exports:** All agent modules for use in routes

---

### 4. SENTRY USAGE

**Configuration (from app/core/config.py):**
```
sentry_dsn: str = "" (optional, leave empty to disable)
sentry_traces_sample_rate: float = 0.1
sentry_profiles_sample_rate: float = 0.1
```

**Initialization (app/main.py):**
- Initialized early (before FastAPI app creation) so automatic integrations active from start
- `sentry_sdk.init()` with:
  - `dsn` from config
  - `environment` from settings.general.environment
  - `traces_sample_rate`, `profiles_sample_rate`
  - `send_default_pii=False` (PHI protection)
  - `enable_logs=True` (Sentry Logs feature)
- Custom `_SkipMiddleware` filter to exclude middleware logs from Sentry
- `SentryLogsHandler` attached to app logger

**Usage Locations:**

1. **`app/main.py`** (3 uses)
   - `sentry_sdk.init()` - Initialization on app startup
   - `sentry_sdk.capture_exception(e)` - Capture database initialization error (line 106)
   - `sentry_sdk.capture_exception(e)` - Capture AI Data Service initialization error (line 121)

2. **`app/middleware/logging_middleware.py`** (3 uses)
   - `sentry_sdk.metrics.distribution()` - Track request duration by endpoint/method/status (line 180-185)
   - `sentry_sdk.metrics.count()` - Count requests by endpoint/method/status (line 186-193)
   - Both metrics include tags: `endpoint` (sanitized path), `method`, `status_class` (1xx/2xx/3xx/4xx/5xx), and for count: `outcome` (success/error)

3. **`app/core/logging_config.py` (SentryLogsHandler)**
   - `import sentry_sdk as _sentry` - Deferred import in handler emit
   - `_sentry.logger.*()` - Forward log records to Sentry Logs with level mapping (debug/info/warning/error/fatal)
   - Structured attributes passed via `attributes` kwarg
   - Sensitive key redaction before forwarding

4. **`app/ai/reference_data_cache.py`** (5 uses)
   - `sentry_sdk.metrics.gauge()` - Memory metrics:
     - `cache.snp_count` - Count of all SNPs
     - `cache.tag_count` - Count of all tags
     - `cache.study_count` - Count of all studies
     - `cache.intervention_count` - Count of all interventions
     - `cache.snp_interaction_count` - Count of SNP interactions

5. **`app/routes/ai_router.py`** (19 uses)
   - `sentry_sdk.metrics.count()` - Event counters:
     - `ai.concerns_chat.started` with `has_files` attribute (line 235-236)
     - `ai.genomic_interpretation.started`
     - `ai.genomic_interpretation.completed`
   - `sentry_sdk.metrics.distribution()` - Latency metrics for various endpoints:
     - `ai.genomic_interpretation.duration_ms`
     - `ai.clinical_discussion.duration_ms`
     - `ai.clinical_discussion_stream.duration_ms`
     - `ai.clinical_chat_stream.duration_ms`
     - `ai.clinician_summary_plan.duration_ms`
     - `ai.prevalence_view.duration_ms`
     - `ai.interventions.duration_ms`
     - `ai.action_plan_commentary.duration_ms`

**Integration Features:**
- **Automatic:** FastAPI integration captures unhandled exceptions and request/response data
- **Distributed Tracing:** `traces_sample_rate=0.1` (10% of requests)
- **Profiling:** `profiles_sample_rate=0.1` (10% of sampled traces)
- **Structured Logs:** All app logs forwarded to Sentry Logs with structured fields
- **Custom Metrics:** Distribution (latency) and Count (events) metrics for AI operations
- **PHI Protection:** `send_default_pii=False` + SensitiveDataFilter redacts sensitive fields before logging

---

### 5. BACKGROUND TASKS & SCHEDULED JOBS

**Single Background Task:**

**`CacheRefreshTask`** (class in `/app/ai/cache_refresh_task.py`)
- **Trigger:** Periodic asyncio task
- **Interval:** 300 seconds (5 minutes) by default, configurable at instantiation
- **Lifecycle:** Started in app lifespan during startup, stopped on shutdown
- **Action:** Calls `ReferenceDataCache.reload()` to refresh all R&D reference data
- **Error Handling:** Logs error but does not crash if reload fails
- **Pattern:** Uses `asyncio.create_task()` for background execution
- **Cancellation:** Gracefully cancels on shutdown with try/except asyncio.CancelledError

**No Scheduled Jobs/Cron:** Application uses periodic polling (asyncio.sleep) rather than cron-based scheduling.

---

### 6. DATABASE SETUP

**Database Type:** PostgreSQL (async)

**Connection:**
- **ORM:** SQLAlchemy with async support (`sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio`)
- **Driver:** asyncpg
- **Connection String:** Configured via `POSTGRES_URL` env var
  - Default: `postgresql+asyncpg://intellxx-admin:qwerty@localhost:5432/intellxxdna`

**Database Configuration (app/core/config.py):**
```python
postgres_url: str = "postgresql+asyncpg://..."
postgres_search_path: str = "dev_data,snp_data" (default)
pool_size: int = 10 (default)
max_overflow: int = 20 (default)
echo_sql: bool = False (default, logs SQL when True)
```

**Connection Management (app/db/postgres.py):**
- **Class:** `PostgresDatabase` - Instance-based gateway
- **Lifecycle:**
  - `connect()` - Creates async engine + session factory (called in app lifespan on startup)
  - `disconnect()` - Disposes engine (called in app lifespan on shutdown)
  - `health_check()` - Verifies connection with `SELECT 1` query
- **Session Management:**
  - `transaction()` - Async context manager for transactional queries
  - `fetch_one()`, `fetch_all()`, `execute()` - Query execution methods
- **Configuration:**
  - Pool: 10 connections, 20 overflow (configurable)
  - Pre-ping: True (verifies connection before reuse)
  - Search path: "dev_data,snp_data" (set per-connection)

**ORM Entities (app/entities/):**
- **Patient Data:** `Patient`, `PatientResult`, `Session` (app/entities/)
- **SNP Data:** `SNP`, `SnpDetail`, `SnpFact`, `SnpFactOddsRatio`, `SnpFactTag`, `SnpFactStudy` (app/entities/snp/)
- **Interventions:** `Intervention`, `InterventionSnpAssociation`, `InterventionSnpAssocStudy`, `InterventionFact` (app/entities/interventions/)
- **SNP Interactions:** `SnpInteraction`, `InteractionSnp`, `InteractionOddsRatio`, `InteractionTag` (app/entities/snp/interaction/)
- **Reference Data:** `Tag`, `TagsAssociation`, `Study` (app/entities/)
- **Tiers:** `SnpInterventionTier` (app/entities/snp/)

**Health Check:**
- Endpoint: `GET /health`
- Verifies: PostgreSQL + OpenAI service availability
- Called on app startup to confirm database is reachable

**Database Repositories (app/repository/):**
- `init_repository(pg_db)` - Initialize all repository layers on app startup
- Pattern: Async repositories for patient, SNP, tag, intervention data access

---

## Summary Statistics

- **Total Routes:** 17 (12 AI, 4 Data, 1 Health/Debug)
- **Agent Count:** 5 (Concerns Extractor, Clinical Discussion, Clinical Chat, Clinician Summary Plan, Action Plan Commentary)
- **Pipeline Count:** 3 (Genomic Interpretation, Interventions, Prevalence View)
- **MLflow Integration:** 9 core functions (config, session_run, agent_run, trace_span, logging, artifact, token, prompt registry)
- **Sentry Metrics:** 19 metric emissions (counts + distributions)
- **Background Tasks:** 1 (cache refresh every 5 minutes)
- **Database:** PostgreSQL with async SQLAlchemy, 10-20 connection pool
- **Entities:** 20+ ORM models across patient, SNP, intervention, interaction domains

---

This completes the thorough research-only exploration of the intell-lexxi-backend codebase.

5. **`POST /ai/clinical-discussion-stream`** - Streaming version of Clinical Discussion Agent. Returns SSE stream of discussion text chunks.

6. **`POST /ai/clinical-chat-stream`** - Streaming Clinical Chat Agent. Conversational interface for clinicians to ask follow-up questions about genomic results with full context.

7. **`POST /ai/clinician-summary-plan`** - Generates opening cards of patient-facing action plan: one Overview card plus one card per concern. Single LLM call with structured output.

8. **`POST /ai/interventions`** - Interventions Rules Engine. Resolves tiered interventions from R&D Tool data based on clinician-selected SNPs per concern.

9. **`POST /ai/action-plan-commentary-data`** - Assembles payload for action plan commentary. Builds selected SNPs and interventions structures for commentary generation.

10. **`POST /ai/action-plan-commentary`** - Generates patient-facing commentary from selected SNPs and interventions using Action Plan Commentary agent (gpt-5.4).

11. **`POST /ai/refresh-cache`** - Manually trigger cache reload of all reference data (tags, SNPs, interventions, studies, interactions).

12. **`POST /ai/chat-stream`** - Generic chat streaming endpoint. Used by UC6 (Chat interface).

#### `/app/routes/data_router.py` (prefix: `/data`)
Patient and genomic data queries:

1. **`GET /data/patients`** - Paginated list of patients with optional search and sorting. Query params: `search` (optional), `page` (1-based), `per_page` (1-100, default 10), `sort_order` (asc|desc). Returns `PaginatedResponse[Patient]`.

2. **`GET /data/patient/{patient_id}`** - Detailed patient information for a specific patient. Returns `Patient` model.

3. **`GET /data/patient/{patient_id}/results`** - Genetic results for a specific patient. Returns `PatientResults` containing gene_results array.

4. **`GET /data/concerns`** - All available clinical concerns. Returns `ConcernsResponse` with concerns array.

#### `/app/routes/misc_router.py`
Utility and health routes:

1. **`GET /health`** - Health check endpoint. Verifies PostgreSQL and OpenAI service availability. Returns `HealthCheckResponse`.

2. **`GET /sentry-debug`** - (Non-production only) Debug endpoint that triggers deliberate ZeroDivisionError to verify Sentry integration.

---

### 2. MLflow INTEGRATION

**File:** `/app/ai/mlflow_tracker.py` (21.6 KB comprehensive module)

**Architecture:**
- **Two independent flags:** `MLFLOW_ENABLED` (master switch) and `MLFLOW_TRACING_ENABLED` (distributed tracing only)
- **Auto-tracing:** Uses `mlflow.openai.autolog()` to capture every OpenAI/Agents SDK call with zero per-call instrumentation
- **Async trace I/O:** Non-blocking background thread for trace flushing (env: `MLFLOW_ENABLE_ASYNC_TRACE_LOGGING=true`)
- **Session-based hierarchy:** Parent `session_run()` groups related `agent_run()` children

**Core Functions:**

1. **`configure()`** - One-time startup initialization. Reads settings:
   - `mlflow_enabled`, `mlflow_tracking_uri`, `mlflow_experiment_name`, `mlflow_tracing_enabled`
   - Optional basic auth: `mlflow_username`, `mlflow_password` (writes to env vars)
   - Sets experiment tags (environment metadata) and enables autolog

2. **`session_run()`** - Context manager for parent MLflow runs. Groups all agent calls from one patient session under single collapsible entry in UI

3. **`agent_run(run_name, params, tags=None)`** - Context manager for individual agent invocation. Logs params, auto-records `latency_ms` on exit. Nests under active session_run when open. Yields mutable metrics dict

4. **`trace_span(name, attributes=None, request_preview=None)`** - Manual distributed tracing parent span. Groups auto-logged child spans. Links to HTTP correlation ID via `mlflow.trace.session` metadata key

5. **`update_trace_preview(response_preview)`** - Sets response preview text on active trace (shown in MLflow UI)

6. **`log_text(text, filename)`** - Log plain-text artifact to active run

7. **`log_dict(data, filename)`** - Log JSON artifact to active run

8. **`log_token_usage(result)`** - Extract and log token counts from OpenAI Agents SDK result. Logs `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`, `total_tokens` as metrics

9. **`ensure_prompt(name, template, commit_message="", tags=None)`** - Register prompt in MLflow Prompt Registry. Compares against latest version; creates new version only if text changed. Returns version number or None on failure

**Configuration (from app/core/config.py):**
- `mlflow_enabled: bool = True` (default enabled)
- `mlflow_tracing_enabled: bool = True` (default enabled)
- `mlflow_tracking_uri: str = "https://api-dev.ixxdna.com/mlflow/"`
- `mlflow_experiment_name: str = "lexxi-ai-agents"`
- `mlflow_username: str = ""` (optional)
- `mlflow_password: str = ""` (optional)

**Initialization:** Called in `app/main.py` lifespan during app startup. Also registers 4 agent system prompts:
- `init_clinical_discussion_prompt()` 
- `init_clinical_chat_prompt()`
- `init_clinician_summary_plan_prompt()`
- `init_concerns_extractor_prompt()`

**Agent Usage:** Imported and used in:
- `/app/ai/agent_extract_concern.py` - Concerns extractor agent (uses agent_run, log_token_usage, trace_span)
- `/app/ai/agent_clinical_discussion.py` - Clinical discussion (session_run, agent_run, trace_span, log_text, log_token_usage)
- `/app/ai/agent_clinical_chat.py` - Clinical chat (agent_run, log_text, log_token_usage)
- `/app/ai/agent_clinician_summary_plan.py` - Summary plan (agent_run, log_text, log_token_usage, trace_span)

---

### 3. ALL AI AGENTS/PIPELINES

**Directory:** `/app/ai/` contains 13 core files

#### **Agents (OpenAI Agents SDK-based, conversational):**

1. **`agent_extract_concern.py` (14.6 KB)**
   - **Purpose:** UC1 (Topic/Concern Selection) — Multi-turn conversational concern extraction from clinician input
   - **Features:** File attachments (PDF/DOCX/TXT), streaming SSE, structured concern output with source tags
   - **Model:** gpt-4o, temperature 0.7, max 1000 tokens
   - **Key Functions:**
     - `run_concerns_stream(conversation, available_concerns)` - Async generator streaming concern extraction
     - `init_prompt()` - Registers prompt in MLflow Registry on startup
   - **Source Tags:** `patient_concerns`, `family_history`, `genomic_results`
   - **Integration:** Uses mlflow_tracker for tracing and prompt registry

2. **`agent_clinical_discussion.py` (21.9 KB)**
   - **Purpose:** Generates clinician-oriented discussion for patient SNP results per concern
   - **Features:** SNP obfuscation ({SNP-X} placeholders), concurrent concern processing, streaming support
   - **Model:** gpt-4o, temperature 0.5, max 1200 tokens
   - **Key Functions:**
     - `run_discussions_concurrent(concerns_data, snps_by_rsid, cache)` - Process multiple concerns concurrently
     - `run_discussions_stream(message, concerns_data, snps_by_rsid, cache)` - Streaming variant
     - `init_prompt()` - Registers prompt at startup
   - **Post-processing:** Normalizes {SNP-X} placeholders, strips leaked rs_ids
   - **Integration:** session_run + agent_run nesting for parent/child run hierarchy

3. **`agent_clinical_chat.py` (16.1 KB)**
   - **Purpose:** Streaming conversational assistant for clinicians to ask follow-up questions about genomic results
   - **Features:** Context-aware (receives prior genomic interpretation + discussion), SNP obfuscation, streaming
   - **Model:** gpt-4o, temperature 0.5, max 1000 tokens
   - **Key Functions:**
     - `run_chat_stream(message, context, snps_by_rsid, tag_by_name)` - Async generator for chat stream
     - `init_prompt()` - Registers prompt
   - **Integration:** Shares obfuscation scheme with clinical_discussion

4. **`agent_clinician_summary_plan.py` (13 KB)**
   - **Purpose:** Generates opening cards of patient-facing action plan (Overview + per-concern cards)
   - **Features:** Single LLM call (faster than concurrent), global SNP mapping, structured output_type
   - **Model:** gpt-4o, temperature 0.5, max 900 tokens
   - **Key Functions:**
     - `run(concerns_data, snps_by_rsid, tag_by_name)` - Single Runner.run() with output_type
     - `init_prompt()` - Registers prompt
   - **Output:** Pydantic model with overview_card + concern_cards

5. **`agent_action_plan_commentary.py` (3.4 KB)**
   - **Purpose:** Generates patient-facing commentary from clinician-selected SNPs and interventions
   - **Features:** Pure AI layer, receives all data via payload
   - **Model:** gpt-5.4, temperature 0.2, max 1600 tokens
   - **Key Functions:**
     - `generate_action_plan_commentary_from_payload(payload)` - Single Runner.run()
   - **Post-processing:** Removes leaked rsIDs

#### **Pipelines (Deterministic Rules Engines — pure logic, no I/O):**

6. **`lexxi_pipeline_genomic_interpretation.py` (111 KB)**
   - **Purpose:** Core deterministic Rules Engine v5 for genomic interpretation
   - **Architecture:** Pure-logic class, zero DB calls. Same input → same output always.
   - **Rules Loaded:**
     - `rules/disabled_snps.json` - SNPs to filter out
     - `rules/fundamentals.json` - Fundamental concept synonyms
     - `rules/critical_snps.json` - SNPs not to miss
   - **Key Features:**
     - Tag walking (topic → associated_tags, depth 1)
     - SNP matching (unique_tags + facts.tags)
     - Prevalence from MAF (Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium)
     - 4 prioritization groups
     - Variant merging (same gene, within 10% prevalence)
     - Key Point (AI) fact extraction
     - SNP interactions support
     - Wellness special case
   - **Key Class:** `LexxiPipelineGenomicInterpretation(snps, tags, tag_by_id, patient)`
   - **References:** Lexxi Rulebook §5-9

7. **`lexxi_pipeline_interventions.py` (20.9 KB)**
   - **Purpose:** Rules Engine for resolving tiered interventions from selected SNPs per concern
   - **Architecture:** Pure logic, zero I/O, deterministic
   - **Data Model:** Maps SNP.tier_information_by_tag[tag_id] → interventions with tier levels
   - **Key Class:** `LexxiPipelineInterventions(snps, interventions, ...)`
   - **Output:** `InterventionsResponse` with per-concern intervention tiers
   - **References:** Lexxi Rulebook §12 (Intervention Engine)

8. **`lexxi_pipeline_prevalence_view.py` (23.8 KB)**
   - **Purpose:** Prevalence View pipeline (pv-v1) — ALL low-prevalence SNPs in single table
   - **Architecture:** Pure logic, decoupled from genomic_interpretation
   - **Key Differences:** No concerns, no tag walking, no 4 selection rules, selection by prevalence ascending
   - **Features:** Matches highest-OR fact per SNP, filtered by disabled SNPs
   - **Key Class:** `LexxiPipelinePrevalenceView(snps, ...)`
   - **Output:** `PrevalenceViewResponse` with SNP results ordered by prevalence
   - **References:** Lexxi UX §5

#### **Data Services & Cache:**

9. **`reference_data_cache.py` (19.7 KB)**
   - **Purpose:** In-memory cache of all R&D Tool reference data (tags, SNPs, interventions, studies, interactions)
   - **Features:**
     - Lock-protected reload (in-flight requests serve previous snapshot while reload in progress)
     - Reconstructs nested document shapes from PostgreSQL normalized tables
     - Two-phase parallel loading (studies+tags first, then SNPs+interventions+interactions)
     - Memory metrics via Sentry: cache.snp_count, cache.tag_count, cache.study_count, cache.intervention_count, cache.snp_interaction_count
   - **Key Methods:**
     - `ensure_loaded()` - Pre-load all data (idempotent)
     - `reload()` - Full reload without serving partial state
   - **Loaded Entities:** Tags, SNPs, SnpDetails, SnpInteractions, Interventions, Studies
   - **Provides:** `all_snps`, `tag_by_name`, `tag_by_id`, `all_interventions`, `all_studies`, `snp_interactions`

10. **`ai_data_service.py` (2.8 KB)**
    - **Purpose:** Patient-specific data access layer (async queries for patient records, genotypes, age)
    - **Key Methods:**
      - `get_patient_by_id(patient_id)` - Fetch patient record with dob as datetime
      - `get_patient_genotypes(patient_id)` - Get gene_results as gene_id → {genotype, result_value, prevalence} dict
      - `get_patient_age(patient)` - Calculate age from date_of_birth
    - **DB Entity Mapping:** Patient, PatientResult via SQLAlchemy ORM

#### **Supporting:**

11. **`cache_refresh_task.py` (1.7 KB)**
    - **Purpose:** Periodic background task for reloading reference data cache
    - **Class:** `CacheRefreshTask` — asyncio-based periodic task manager
    - **Default Interval:** 300 seconds (5 minutes)
    - **Lifecycle:** `start()` schedules task, `stop()` cancels and waits cleanly
    - **Usage:** Created in app lifespan, runs during app lifetime

12. **`chat.py` (1.1 KB)**
    - **Purpose:** Generic chat functionality wrapper (minimal, likely legacy or utility)

13. **`ai/__init__.py` (79 bytes)**
    - **Exports:** All agent modules for use in routes

---

### 4. SENTRY USAGE

**Configuration (from app/core/config.py):**
```
sentry_dsn: str = "" (optional, leave empty to disable)
sentry_traces_sample_rate: float = 0.1
sentry_profiles_sample_rate: float = 0.1
```

**Initialization (app/main.py):**
- Initialized early (before FastAPI app creation) so automatic integrations active from start
- `sentry_sdk.init()` with:
  - `dsn` from config
  - `environment` from settings.general.environment
  - `traces_sample_rate`, `profiles_sample_rate`
  - `send_default_pii=False` (PHI protection)
  - `enable_logs=True` (Sentry Logs feature)
- Custom `_SkipMiddleware` filter to exclude middleware logs from Sentry
- `SentryLogsHandler` attached to app logger

**Usage Locations:**

1. **`app/main.py`** (3 uses)
   - `sentry_sdk.init()` - Initialization on app startup
   - `sentry_sdk.capture_exception(e)` - Capture database initialization error (line 106)
   - `sentry_sdk.capture_exception(e)` - Capture AI Data Service initialization error (line 121)

2. **`app/middleware/logging_middleware.py`** (3 uses)
   - `sentry_sdk.metrics.distribution()` - Track request duration by endpoint/method/status (line 180-185)
   - `sentry_sdk.metrics.count()` - Count requests by endpoint/method/status (line 186-193)
   - Both metrics include tags: `endpoint` (sanitized path), `method`, `status_class` (1xx/2xx/3xx/4xx/5xx), and for count: `outcome` (success/error)

3. **`app/core/logging_config.py` (SentryLogsHandler)**
   - `import sentry_sdk as _sentry` - Deferred import in handler emit
   - `_sentry.logger.*()` - Forward log records to Sentry Logs with level mapping (debug/info/warning/error/fatal)
   - Structured attributes passed via `attributes` kwarg
   - Sensitive key redaction before forwarding

4. **`app/ai/reference_data_cache.py`** (5 uses)
   - `sentry_sdk.metrics.gauge()` - Memory metrics:
     - `cache.snp_count` - Count of all SNPs
     - `cache.tag_count` - Count of all tags
     - `cache.study_count` - Count of all studies
     - `cache.intervention_count` - Count of all interventions
     - `cache.snp_interaction_count` - Count of SNP interactions

5. **`app/routes/ai_router.py`** (19 uses)
   - `sentry_sdk.metrics.count()` - Event counters:
     - `ai.concerns_chat.started` with `has_files` attribute (line 235-236)
     - `ai.genomic_interpretation.started`
     - `ai.genomic_interpretation.completed`
   - `sentry_sdk.metrics.distribution()` - Latency metrics for various endpoints:
     - `ai.genomic_interpretation.duration_ms`
     - `ai.clinical_discussion.duration_ms`
     - `ai.clinical_discussion_stream.duration_ms`
     - `ai.clinical_chat_stream.duration_ms`
     - `ai.clinician_summary_plan.duration_ms`
     - `ai.prevalence_view.duration_ms`
     - `ai.interventions.duration_ms`
     - `ai.action_plan_commentary.duration_ms`

**Integration Features:**
- **Automatic:** FastAPI integration captures unhandled exceptions and request/response data
- **Distributed Tracing:** `traces_sample_rate=0.1` (10% of requests)
- **Profiling:** `profiles_sample_rate=0.1` (10% of sampled traces)
- **Structured Logs:** All app logs forwarded to Sentry Logs with structured fields
- **Custom Metrics:** Distribution (latency) and Count (events) metrics for AI operations
- **PHI Protection:** `send_default_pii=False` + SensitiveDataFilter redacts sensitive fields before logging

---

### 5. BACKGROUND TASKS & SCHEDULED JOBS

**Single Background Task:**

**`CacheRefreshTask`** (class in `/app/ai/cache_refresh_task.py`)
- **Trigger:** Periodic asyncio task
- **Interval:** 300 seconds (5 minutes) by default, configurable at instantiation
- **Lifecycle:** Started in app lifespan during startup, stopped on shutdown
- **Action:** Calls `ReferenceDataCache.reload()` to refresh all R&D reference data
- **Error Handling:** Logs error but does not crash if reload fails
- **Pattern:** Uses `asyncio.create_task()` for background execution
- **Cancellation:** Gracefully cancels on shutdown with try/except asyncio.CancelledError

**No Scheduled Jobs/Cron:** Application uses periodic polling (asyncio.sleep) rather than cron-based scheduling.

---

### 6. DATABASE SETUP

**Database Type:** PostgreSQL (async)

**Connection:**
- **ORM:** SQLAlchemy with async support (`sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio`)
- **Driver:** asyncpg
- **Connection String:** Configured via `POSTGRES_URL` env var
  - Default: `postgresql+asyncpg://intellxx-admin:qwerty@localhost:5432/intellxxdna`

**Database Configuration (app/core/config.py):**
```python
postgres_url: str = "postgresql+asyncpg://..."
postgres_search_path: str = "dev_data,snp_data" (default)
pool_size: int = 10 (default)
max_overflow: int = 20 (default)
echo_sql: bool = False (default, logs SQL when True)
```

**Connection Management (app/db/postgres.py):**
- **Class:** `PostgresDatabase` - Instance-based gateway
- **Lifecycle:**
  - `connect()` - Creates async engine + session factory (called in app lifespan on startup)
  - `disconnect()` - Disposes engine (called in app lifespan on shutdown)
  - `health_check()` - Verifies connection with `SELECT 1` query
- **Session Management:**
  - `transaction()` - Async context manager for transactional queries
  - `fetch_one()`, `fetch_all()`, `execute()` - Query execution methods
- **Configuration:**
  - Pool: 10 connections, 20 overflow (configurable)
  - Pre-ping: True (verifies connection before reuse)
  - Search path: "dev_data,snp_data" (set per-connection)

**ORM Entities (app/entities/):**
- **Patient Data:** `Patient`, `PatientResult`, `Session` (app/entities/)
- **SNP Data:** `SNP`, `SnpDetail`, `SnpFact`, `SnpFactOddsRatio`, `SnpFactTag`, `SnpFactStudy` (app/entities/snp/)
- **Interventions:** `Intervention`, `InterventionSnpAssociation`, `InterventionSnpAssocStudy`, `InterventionFact` (app/entities/interventions/)
- **SNP Interactions:** `SnpInteraction`, `InteractionSnp`, `InteractionOddsRatio`, `InteractionTag` (app/entities/snp/interaction/)
- **Reference Data:** `Tag`, `TagsAssociation`, `Study` (app/entities/)
- **Tiers:** `SnpInterventionTier` (app/entities/snp/)

**Health Check:**
- Endpoint: `GET /health`
- Verifies: PostgreSQL + OpenAI service availability
- Called on app startup to confirm database is reachable

**Database Repositories (app/repository/):**
- `init_repository(pg_db)` - Initialize all repository layers on app startup
- Pattern: Async repositories for patient, SNP, tag, intervention data access

---

## Summary Statistics

- **Total Routes:** 17 (12 AI, 4 Data, 1 Health/Debug)
- **Agent Count:** 5 (Concerns Extractor, Clinical Discussion, Clinical Chat, Clinician Summary Plan, Action Plan Commentary)
- **Pipeline Count:** 3 (Genomic Interpretation, Interventions, Prevalence View)
- **MLflow Integration:** 9 core functions (config, session_run, agent_run, trace_span, logging, artifact, token, prompt registry)
- **Sentry Metrics:** 19 metric emissions (counts + distributions)
- **Background Tasks:** 1 (cache refresh every 5 minutes)
- **Database:** PostgreSQL with async SQLAlchemy, 10-20 connection pool
- **Entities:** 20+ ORM models across patient, SNP, intervention, interaction domains

---

This completes the thorough research-only exploration of the intell-lexxi-backend codebase.

content.text:
Here's the summary:

---

**`intell-lexxi-backend` — Status Update**

Backend service powering Lexxi's AI-assisted genomic interpretation platform. Built on FastAPI + async PostgreSQL, it exposes 17 endpoints across three layers: patient data access, AI agent pipelines, and reference data management.

**AI Pipelines**
Five conversational agents (OpenAI Agents SDK / GPT-4o): Concern Extractor, Clinical Discussion, Clinical Chat, Clinician Summary Plan, and Action Plan Commentary. Three deterministic rules engines handle Genomic Interpretation (v5), Interventions resolution, and Prevalence View — all pure logic, zero I/O, same input always produces the same output. A background task refreshes the in-memory R&D reference data cache (tags, SNPs, interventions, studies) every 5 minutes.

**MLflow Integration**
Every agent call is tracked end-to-end. `mlflow.openai.autolog()` captures all OpenAI/Agents SDK calls with zero per-call instrumentation. Parent `session_run` → child `agent_run` hierarchy groups all calls from a single patient session in the MLflow UI. Latency, token usage (input/output/total), and text artifacts are logged per run. Four agent system prompts are registered in the MLflow Prompt Registry on startup — a new version is only created if the prompt text has changed since the last deploy.

**Sentry Integration**
Full observability stack:
- **Error tracking** — unhandled exceptions auto-captured via FastAPI integration; startup failures explicitly captured with `capture_exception`
- **Performance** — 10% of requests traced and profiled
- **Metrics** — request duration and count per endpoint/method/status (middleware), plus per-AI-operation latency and event counters (19 metric emissions total), and cache size gauges after every reload
- **Structured Logs** — all app-level logs forwarded to Sentry Logs via a custom handler bridge; HTTP request/response logs are explicitly excluded to avoid noise
- **PHI protection** — `send_default_pii=False`, sensitive field redaction in logs, numeric IDs stripped from URL paths before any log or metric emission

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""HTTP request/response logging middleware with correlation ID propagation."""

import json
import logging
import re
import time
import uuid
from contextvars import ContextVar

import sentry_sdk
from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware
from starlette.requests import Request
from starlette.responses import Response

_METRIC_PATH_ID_RE = re.compile(r"(?<=/)\d+(?=/|$)")


def _sanitize_metric_path(path: str) -> str:
    """Replace inline numeric IDs with {id} to keep metric cardinality low."""
    return _METRIC_PATH_ID_RE.sub("{id}", path)


logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# Async-safe context variable — carries the correlation ID across the full request lifecycle
_correlation_id_ctx: ContextVar[str] = ContextVar("correlation_id", default="")

CORRELATION_ID_HEADER = "X-Correlation-ID"

# Endpoints excluded from verbose INFO logging (still logged at DEBUG)
_QUIET_PATHS = frozenset(["/health", "/metrics", "/favicon.ico"])

# Path prefixes where request body is NEVER logged regardless of log level
# (passwords, tokens, credentials)
_SENSITIVE_PATH_PREFIXES = ("/auth/",)

# Methods that can carry a body worth logging
_BODY_METHODS = frozenset(["POST", "PUT", "PATCH"])

# Maximum bytes read from the body before truncating
_BODY_MAX_BYTES = 4_096


def get_correlation_id() -> str:
    """Return the correlation ID for the current request context."""
    return _correlation_id_ctx.get()


def _is_sensitive_path(path: str) -> bool:
    return any(path.startswith(prefix) for prefix in _SENSITIVE_PATH_PREFIXES)


async def _read_request_body(request: Request) -> str | None:
    """Read and return the request body as a loggable string.

    Starlette caches the raw bytes in request._body after the first read,
    so the route handler will still receive the full body normally.

    Returns None if the body should not be logged (binary, empty, etc.).
    """
    content_type = request.headers.get("content-type", "")

    # Skip file uploads and other binary payloads
    if "multipart/form-data" in content_type:
        return "<multipart — skipped>"

    raw: bytes = await request.body()
    if not raw:
        return None

    truncated = len(raw) > _BODY_MAX_BYTES
    chunk = raw[:_BODY_MAX_BYTES]

    if "application/json" in content_type:
        try:
            parsed = json.loads(chunk)
            suffix = " [truncated]" if truncated else ""
            return json.dumps(parsed) + suffix
        except (json.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError):
            pass

    try:
        text = chunk.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
        return text + (" [truncated]" if truncated else "")
    except Exception:
        return f"<binary {len(raw)} bytes>"


class RequestLoggingMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):
    """Log every HTTP request and its response with structured fields and elapsed time.

    Each request receives a unique correlation ID (from the incoming
    X-Correlation-ID header if present, otherwise generated). The ID is:
    - Stored in a ContextVar so any logger in the call stack can read it
    - Returned in the X-Correlation-ID response header for client tracing

    Request body logging:
    - Only active when the effective log level is DEBUG
    - Never logged for sensitive paths (e.g. /auth/*)
    - Skipped for multipart/form-data (file uploads)
    - Truncated at 4 096 bytes

    Log fields emitted:
        Request start : correlation_id, http_method, http_path, http_query,
                        client_ip, user_agent, [request_body]
        Request end   : correlation_id, http_method, http_path, http_status,
                        duration_ms, client_ip
    """

    async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next) -> Response:
        correlation_id = request.headers.get(CORRELATION_ID_HEADER) or str(uuid.uuid4())
        ctx_token = _correlation_id_ctx.set(correlation_id)

        path = request.url.path
        method = request.method
        client_ip = request.client.host if request.client else "unknown"
        user_agent = request.headers.get("user-agent", "")
        query = request.url.query or ""
        is_quiet = path in _QUIET_PATHS

        log_extra_base = {
            "correlation_id": correlation_id,
            "http_method": method,
            "http_path": path,
            "client_ip": client_ip,
        }

        if not is_quiet:
            request_extra: dict = {
                **log_extra_base,
                "http_query": query,
                "user_agent": user_agent,
            }

            # Body logging: DEBUG level + non-sensitive path + body-carrying method
            if (
                logger.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG)
                and method in _BODY_METHODS
                and not _is_sensitive_path(path)
            ):
                body = await _read_request_body(request)
                if body is not None:
                    request_extra["request_body"] = body

            logger.info("Request started", extra=request_extra)

        start = time.perf_counter()
        status_code = 500

        try:
            response: Response = await call_next(request)
            status_code = response.status_code
        except Exception:
            logger.exception(
                "Unhandled exception during request processing",
                extra=log_extra_base,
            )
            raise
        finally:
            duration_ms = round((time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000, 2)
            _correlation_id_ctx.reset(ctx_token)

            if not is_quiet:
                log_fn = logger.warning if status_code >= 400 else logger.info
                log_fn(
                    "Request completed",
                    extra={
                        **log_extra_base,
                        "http_status": status_code,
                        "duration_ms": duration_ms,
                    },
                )

                metric_attrs = {
                    "endpoint": _sanitize_metric_path(path),
                    "method": method,
                    "status_class": f"{status_code // 100}xx",
                }
                sentry_sdk.metrics.distribution(
                    "api.request.duration_ms",
                    duration_ms,
                    unit="millisecond",
                    tags=metric_attrs,
                )
                sentry_sdk.metrics.count(
                    "api.request.count",
                    1,
                    tags={
                        **metric_attrs,
                        "outcome": "success" if status_code < 400 else "error",
                    },
                )
            else:
                logger.debug(
                    "Health check",
                    extra={**log_extra_base, "http_status": status_code},
                )

        response.headers[CORRELATION_ID_HEADER] = correlation_id
        return response

content.text:
Clean. Here's a summary of everything fixed across both projects:

| Bug | Root cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `distribution() got unexpected kwarg 'attributes'` | `sentry_sdk.metrics` uses `tags=`, not `attributes=` | Replaced in both middlewares |
| `module has no attribute 'count'` | API is `sentry_sdk.metrics.incr()`, not `count()` | Replaced in both middlewares + 3 calls in `ai_router.py` |
| `attributes=` on `incr`/`distribution` in `ai_router.py` | Same `tags=` vs `attributes=` confusion | Replaced all 19 occurrences |

**Rule to remember:** `sentry_sdk.metrics.*` always uses `tags=`. `attributes=` is only for `sentry_sdk.logger.*`.

1	"""HTTP request/response logging middleware with correlation ID propagation."""
2	
3	import json
4	import logging
5	import re
6	import time
7	import uuid
8	from contextvars import ContextVar
9	
10	import sentry_sdk
11	from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware
12	from starlette.requests import Request
13	from starlette.responses import Response
14	
15	_METRIC_PATH_ID_RE = re.compile(r"(?<=/)\d+(?=/|$)")
16	
17	
18	def _sanitize_metric_path(path: str) -> str:
19	    """Replace inline numeric IDs with {id} to keep metric cardinality low."""
20	    return _METRIC_PATH_ID_RE.sub("{id}", path)
21	
22	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
23	
24	# Async-safe context variable — carries the correlation ID across the full request lifecycle
25	_correlation_id_ctx: ContextVar[str] = ContextVar("correlation_id", default="")
26	
27	CORRELATION_ID_HEADER = "X-Correlation-ID"
28	
29	# Endpoints excluded from verbose INFO logging (still logged at DEBUG)
30	_QUIET_PATHS = frozenset(["/health", "/metrics", "/favicon.ico"])
31	
32	# Path prefixes where request body is NEVER logged regardless of log level
33	# (passwords, tokens, credentials)
34	_SENSITIVE_PATH_PREFIXES = ("/auth/",)
35	
36	# Methods that can carry a body worth logging
37	_BODY_METHODS = frozenset(["POST", "PUT", "PATCH"])
38	
39	# Maximum bytes read from the body before truncating
40	_BODY_MAX_BYTES = 4_096
41	
42	
43	def get_correlation_id() -> str:
44	    """Return the correlation ID for the current request context."""
45	    return _correlation_id_ctx.get()
46	
47	
48	def _is_sensitive_path(path: str) -> bool:
49	    return any(path.startswith(prefix) for prefix in _SENSITIVE_PATH_PREFIXES)
50	
51	
52	async def _read_request_body(request: Request) -> str | None:
53	    """Read and return the request body as a loggable string.
54	
55	    Starlette caches the raw bytes in request._body after the first read,
56	    so the route handler will still receive the full body normally.
57	
58	    Returns None if the body should not be logged (binary, empty, etc.).
59	    """
60	    content_type = request.headers.get("content-type", "")
61	
62	    # Skip file uploads and other binary payloads
63	    if "multipart/form-data" in content_type:
64	        return "<multipart — skipped>"
65	
66	    raw: bytes = await request.body()
67	    if not raw:
68	        return None
69	
70	    truncated = len(raw) > _BODY_MAX_BYTES
71	    chunk = raw[:_BODY_MAX_BYTES]
72	
73	    if "application/json" in content_type:
74	        try:
75	            parsed = json.loads(chunk)
76	            suffix = " [truncated]" if truncated else ""
77	            return json.dumps(parsed) + suffix
78	        except (json.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError):
79	            pass
80	
81	    try:
82	        text = chunk.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
83	        return text + (" [truncated]" if truncated else "")
84	    except Exception:
85	        return f"<binary {len(raw)} bytes>"
86	
87	
88	class RequestLoggingMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):
89	    """Log every HTTP request and its response with structured fields and elapsed time.
90	
91	    Each request receives a unique correlation ID (from the incoming
92	    X-Correlation-ID header if present, otherwise generated). The ID is:
93	    - Stored in a ContextVar so any logger in the call stack can read it
94	    - Returned in the X-Correlation-ID response header for client tracing
95	
96	    Request body logging:
97	    - Only active when the effective log level is DEBUG
98	    - Never logged for sensitive paths (e.g. /auth/*)
99	    - Skipped for multipart/form-data (file uploads)
100	    - Truncated at 4 096 bytes
101	
102	    Log fields emitted:
103	        Request start : correlation_id, http_method, http_path, http_query,
104	                        client_ip, user_agent, [request_body]
105	        Request end   : correlation_id, http_method, http_path, http_status,
106	                        duration_ms, client_ip
107	    """
108	
109	    async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next) -> Response:
110	        correlation_id = (
111	            request.headers.get(CORRELATION_ID_HEADER) or str(uuid.uuid4())
112	        )
113	        ctx_token = _correlation_id_ctx.set(correlation_id)
114	
115	        path = request.url.path
116	        method = request.method
117	        client_ip = request.client.host if request.client else "unknown"
118	        user_agent = request.headers.get("user-agent", "")
119	        query = request.url.query or ""
120	        is_quiet = path in _QUIET_PATHS
121	
122	        log_extra_base = {
123	            "correlation_id": correlation_id,
124	            "http_method": method,
125	            "http_path": path,
126	            "client_ip": client_ip,
127	        }
128	
129	        if not is_quiet:
130	            request_extra: dict = {
131	                **log_extra_base,
132	                "http_query": query,
133	                "user_agent": user_agent,
134	            }
135	
136	            # Body logging: DEBUG level + non-sensitive path + body-carrying method
137	            if (
138	                logger.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG)
139	                and method in _BODY_METHODS
140	                and not _is_sensitive_path(path)
141	            ):
142	                body = await _read_request_body(request)
143	                if body is not None:
144	                    request_extra["request_body"] = body
145	
146	            logger.info("Request started", extra=request_extra)
147	
148	        start = time.perf_counter()
149	        status_code = 500
150	
151	        try:
152	            response: Response = await call_next(request)
153	            status_code = response.status_code
154	        except Exception:
155	            logger.exception(
156	                "Unhandled exception during request processing",
157	                extra=log_extra_base,
158	            )
159	            raise
160	        finally:
161	            duration_ms = round((time.perf_counter() - start) * 1000, 2)
162	            _correlation_id_ctx.reset(ctx_token)
163	
164	            if not is_quiet:
165	                log_fn = logger.warning if status_code >= 400 else logger.info
166	                log_fn(
167	                    "Request completed",
168	                    extra={
169	                        **log_extra_base,
170	                        "http_status": status_code,
171	                        "duration_ms": duration_ms,
172	                    },
173	                )
174	
175	                metric_attrs = {
176	                    "endpoint": _sanitize_metric_path(path),
177	                    "method": method,
178	                    "status_class": f"{status_code // 100}xx",
179	                }
180	                sentry_sdk.metrics.distribution(
181	                    "api.request.duration_ms",
182	                    duration_ms,
183	                    unit="millisecond",
184	                    attributes=metric_attrs,
185	                )
186	                sentry_sdk.metrics.count(
187	                    "api.request.count",
188	                    1,
189	                    attributes={
190	                        **metric_attrs,
191	                        "outcome": "success" if status_code < 400 else "error",
192	                    },
193	                )
194	            else:
195	                logger.debug(
196	                    "Health check",
197	                    extra={**log_extra_base, "http_status": status_code},
198	                )
199	
200	        response.headers[CORRELATION_ID_HEADER] = correlation_id
201	        return response
